rationale: New capability (WS voice round-trip for AgentTalk) but the repo already ships voice.py bot, clients/live.py, and Telegram/MSTeams voice/TTS specs — so this is enrichment of existing STT/TTS/transport primitives, not greenfield.

queries.intent: Read bots/voice.py to understand the existing voice bot abstraction and whether it already does STT->LLM->TTS
Find existing TTS/STT clients and providers (whisper, supertonic, elevenlabs, openai audio, google) across both packages
Locate the AgentTalk / chatbot response object and the response.response / response.data / response.output fields the requester referenced
Read models/outputs.py — the canonical response/output model that TTS must read response.response from
Read clients/live.py — likely the OpenAI realtime/live audio client; determines if realtime STT/TTS already exists
Understand the WebSocket transport layer (human/channels/web.py) used for web HITL long-poll — candidate transport for audio
Find the existing voice specs to reuse their decisions (Telegram TTS reply FEAT-213, msteams-voice, telegram-voice-notes)
Read FEAT-213 telegram-voice-reply-tts spec — closest prior art for the TTS reply path and which provider it picked
Read msteams-voice-support spec — prior art for STT-in / TTS-out round trip over a channel
Find how integrations (telegram/teams) currently transcribe incoming audio (STT entrypoint) to reuse for WS audio
Identify the HTTP/handler layer (aiohttp) that serves chatbots so a new WS voice endpoint can register alongside it
Check the demo agent websocket usage (agents/demo.py) as a concrete WS streaming example
Find any audio loader / audio rendering already present (formdesigner-audio-renderer referenced an audio renderer)
Recent git activity on voice/live/audio code to see what is actively evolving

expected_findings: An existing voice bot abstraction (bots/voice.py) that may already chain STT->LLM->TTS
Existing STT (whisper/openai) and possibly TTS providers to reuse instead of building from scratch
The canonical response model exposing response.response as the speakable text field
A websocket transport (human/channels/web.py and/or handlers) to carry audio frames
Prior-art specs (FEAT-213, msteams-voice-support) establishing provider + payload conventions
